content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 29, 2001

Elvira Vargas, Filing Officer
County of San Diego
Registrar of Voters
5201 Ruffin Road, Suite 1
San Diego, CA 92123-1693

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. W-01-054

Dear Ms. Vargas:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ We decline to provide written assistance to you because your question is hypothetical (Regulation 18329(b)(8).)

The Fair Political Practices Commission is currently in the process of implementing the provisions of Proposition 34, passed by voters in November 2000. We encourage you to monitor our progress on the implementation of Proposition 34 by visiting the Commission's website at, www.fppc.ca.gov, which contains up-to-date information on pending regulatory matters concerning all aspects of the Act. Additionally, the website contains agendas for past and future Commission meetings and instructions on how to obtain relevant materials. Question and Answer sheets designed to address common questions to the Commission are also available. For more particular advice, you may reach a Commission consultant and receive telephone advice by calling 866-ASK-FPPC. While this advice does afford the immunity provided recipients of written advice, the telephone advice is ideal for routine questions that can be answered in a short time.

_______________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. 01-054
Page No. 2

I will also add your name and address to the FPPC list of "Proposition 34 -
Interested Persons" which will periodically notify you of meetings on relevant topics
before the Commission.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Joan Giannetta
Secretary, Legal Division

/jg
I:\AdviceLetrs\01-054

sections.conclusion: We decline to provide written assistance to you because your question is hypothetical (Regulation 18329(b)(8).)
sections.question_synthetic: The filing officer sought informal written advice from the FPPC regarding provisions of the Political Reform Act, likely related to implementation of Proposition 34.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the question posed was hypothetical in nature, but directed the requester to alternative resources including the FPPC website, telephone advice line, and Q&A materials for assistance with Proposition 34 implementation.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with clear conclusion section. No formal 'QUESTION' section header present but the nature of the request is discernible from the letterhead and context. Document shows typical FPPC structure for declining hypothetical questions while providing alternative guidance.

CONCLUSION: We decline to provide written assistance to you because your question is hypothetical (Regulation 18329(b)(8).)

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide written informal assistance to the San Diego County Registrar of Voters regarding the Political Reform Act because her question was hypothetical. The Commission offered alternative resources and added her to the Proposition 34 interested persons list.
